danger_signals.visual_form: House cats (Tigers) and green iguanas (Iggys) rendered as distinct sprites on playfield
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to player and Chirps; contact disperses collected Chirps

danger_signals.visual_form: Chirps scatter visually when chain contacts enemy
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of progress; need to recollect and re-chain dispersed Chirps

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual modifier on specific Chirp sprites (sunglasses drawn on 8x8 pixel chick)
danger_signals.meaning: Unpredictable behavior when dispersed; increased difficulty/danger state

safety_signals.visual_form: Exit platform positioned on screen with door visual
safety_signals.meaning: Safe delivery zone; collecting Chirps here clears round

safety_signals.visual_form: Throwable objects (telephones, flower pots, cups, bottles) visible on playfield; diamonds also present
safety_signals.meaning: Defense mechanism; player can proactively eliminate threats before Chirps are endangered

safety_signals.visual_form: Blue bird sprite with side-to-side run and jump animations
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency to navigate threats and protect Chirps; movement-based safety strategy

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Urban apartment building setting; platforms represent building architecture

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Small chick sprites; some with sunglasses
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Sunglasses variant indicates 'bad' Chirps with unpredictable behavior
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Distributed across playfield platforms; must be guided to exit
Secondary threat type introduced post-development; patrol playfield

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple Chirp sprites visible on platforms, some forming chains behind Flicky
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Chirps follow Flicky in line formation; enemies actively patrol; player must navigate and collect

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Collected Chirps scatter explosively across screen, breaking chain formation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All gathered Chirps separate and resume independent movement; player must restart collection effort

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Chirp sprites vanish upon exit door contact; platform state clears
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stage advances to next level; difficulty increases on repeat cycles

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Chirps fall from top of screen; net visual presented to player
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Flicky attempts catch mechanic rather than collection escort; different win condition

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Background color variations (same architectural theme, different palette); same stage structure repeated
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased enemy aggression/speed; Chirp behavior becomes more chaotic on subsequent loops

environmental_storytelling.example: Urban apartment building background (inspired by Kawasaki's office window view) conveys domestic safety theme; player must return Chirps 'home' to building

Game originally conceived as power lines (from Japanese variety show sparrow song reference), later reframed as apartment building levels; environment reinforces 'protect the children and return them safe to home' emotional core
House cats (Tigers) and iguanas (Iggys) as predators in urban setting; natural predators that would threaten small birds; environmental logic reinforces danger
Throwable objects (phones, pots, cups, bottles) scattered on levels; not all levels have defense items; player must choose whether to use items or avoid enemies
Only 4 unique background designs created initially; color shifts applied to create visual variety across 48+ stages without new assets; communicates progression through repeated-but-distinct spaces

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves side-to-side, jumps to reach platforms, picks up items by walking into them, throws by jumping
Chain formation behind player in single-file line; award bonus points when multiple delivered simultaneously
More chaotic movement patterns when separated from Flicky; designed as 'bad' Chirps to increase difficulty
Can be avoided by standard movement or defeated by thrown items; contact breaks Chirp chains and disperses all collected
Cannot be thrown like other items; provide bonus points only when collected; visual indicator of risk-reward (collecting = exposed to enemies)

notes: Flicky communicates world-state primarily through visual clarity of entity positioning and state. The core design philosophy ('protect the children') manifests through visual contrast: vulnerable yellow Chirps vs. predatory enemy sprites vs. safe blue player character. The sunglasses on 'bad' Chirps is a subtle visual tell for increased unpredictability — behavioral manifestation through character design. The game relies heavily on spatial communication (chain formation, exit location, platform topology) rather than narrative UI. Color palette shifts across 48 levels communicate progression without asset variation. The urban apartment setting grounds the protection/safety narrative visually.
